frameworks.name: longitudinal two-photon calcium imaging
frameworks.one_line: Tracking activity of identified neurons across many sessions in same animal.

frameworks.name: voluntary head-fixation
frameworks.one_line: Magnetic, no-moving-parts engagement replacing mechanical restraint paradigms.

frameworks.name: memory persistence in hippocampus
frameworks.one_line: Motivating problem of how CA1 supports lifetime-stable memory.

core_question: How can the same hippocampal neurons be optically recorded during naturalistic behavior across a large fraction of an animal's lifetime?
key_assumptions: Stable optical access to dorsal CA1 requires head-fixation
Voluntary engagement preserves naturalistic behavioral repertoire
GCaMP6f transgenic expression remains stable long-term
manipulations: Mechanical restraint replaced with magnetic voluntary head-fixation
Standard two-photon collection replaced with novel signal-enhancing scheme
Wild-type imaging replaced with transgenic GCaMP6f rat line in dorsal CA1
key_findings: Animals readily engage and disengage at will, system is failsafe and reliable
Novel two-photon collection scheme increases fluorescence signal
Same dorsal CA1 neurons can be tracked across a large fraction of animals' lives
interpretations: Voluntary head-fixation combined with transgenic GCaMP6f enables routine lifetime-scale single-neuron tracking during complex behavior
